title: Platform Audit Evidence File
description: Standardized evidence format for the modular audit system v2. Every platform audit skill outputs a JSON file conforming to this schema. This is the contract between platform skills and the audit-synthesizer.

meta.description: Audit metadata — who, what, when, how.

client.description: Client display name, e.g. 'Kodiak Leather Co.'

platform.description: Platform identifier. Must match one of the recognized platform keys.

date_range.description: Date range of the data analyzed.

access_level.description: Level of account access available during audit.

auditor_notes.description: Free-text context about data quality, limitations, or anything the synthesizer should know.

account_overview.description: Top-level account metrics. Every numeric value has both a raw number and a display string. Every data point is labeled with its provenance.

campaigns.description: Campaign-level breakdown. Optional — not all platforms use campaign structures (e.g., Klaviyo uses flows, Shopify uses channels).

name.description: Campaign name as shown in the platform.

spend.description: Total spend in the date range.

revenue.description: Total attributed revenue in the date range.

key_signals.description: Machine-readable signal tags for the synthesizer (e.g., 'budget_limited', 'above_target_roas', 'creative_fatigue').

tracking_health.description: Conversion tracking and measurement health. Optional — primarily for ad platforms and GA4.

title.description: Short description of the tracking issue.

evidence.description: What was observed that indicates the issue.

source.description: Where in the platform this was observed.

conversion_actions.description: Inventory of conversion actions / events configured in the platform.

in_goals.description: Whether this action is included in primary conversion goals.

findings.description: Key findings from the audit. Each finding is a discrete observation with supporting evidence.

source.description: Where in the platform this was observed. Required when label is OBSERVED.

significance.description: Why this matters — business impact or strategic implication.

anomalies.description: Unexpected patterns, outliers, or things that don't add up. Optional but valuable for the synthesizer.

diagnosis.description: Platform-level diagnosis — what's the main constraint and what else is contributing.

primary_constraint.description: The single biggest issue or opportunity on this platform.

evidence_refs.description: References back to specific data points or findings that support this diagnosis.

opportunities.description: Prioritized actions. Each opportunity must cite evidence and estimate impact.

action.description: What to do — specific and actionable.

expected_impact.description: Estimated impact — revenue, efficiency, or percentage improvement.

confidence.description: How confident are we in this recommendation.

confidence_reasoning.description: Why we have this confidence level.

cross_channel_signals.description: Signals this platform audit noticed that require cross-platform investigation. The synthesizer picks these up and checks them against other evidence files.

signal.description: What was noticed on this platform.

check_in.description: Which other platforms should investigate this signal.

what_to_look_for.description: Specific question or data point to check on those platforms.

open_questions.description: Questions that couldn't be answered with available data. Flagged for the AM or synthesizer.

data_needed.description: What data would answer this question.

attempted.description: What was tried and why it didn't work.

scoring.description: Platform health score computed by the weighted scoring system. Optional — evidence files without this object still validate. See reference/scoring-system.md for the full algorithm.

platform_score.description: Weighted platform health score (0-100). Computed from category scores × category weights.

grade.description: Letter grade mapped from platform_score. A=90-100, B=75-89, C=60-74, D=40-59, F=0-39.

categories.description: Scoring categories with individual check results. Category weights must sum to 1.0.

name.description: Category name (must match platform's defined categories in scoring-system.md).

weight.description: Category weight as decimal (e.g., 0.25 for 25%). All weights must sum to 1.0.

score.description: Category score (0-100) computed from check results and severity multipliers.

checks_passed.description: Number of checks with PASS result.

checks.description: Individual check results within this category.

id.description: Unique check ID within the platform. Format: {platform-abbrev}-{category-abbrev}-{nn}.

name.description: Human-readable description of what was checked.

result.description: Check result. N_A = could not evaluate (excluded from score calculation).

severity.description: Check severity. Determines multiplier: critical=5.0, high=3.0, medium=1.5, low=0.5.

notes.description: Evidence or context supporting the result.

quick_wins.description: High-severity checks that failed but can be fixed in ≤15 minutes. Sorted by severity × expected_impact descending.

check_id.description: References a check id from the categories array.

description.description: What to fix — specific and actionable.

severity.description: Only critical or high severity checks qualify as Quick Wins.

estimated_minutes.description: Estimated time to implement the fix (must be ≤15).

expected_impact.description: Expected business impact of implementing the fix.

raw_metrics.description: Full data tables the synthesizer may need for deeper analysis. Structure varies by platform — use descriptive keys.

additionalProperties.description: Platform-specific data tables not covered by the named keys above.

evidence_label.description: Provenance label for every data point. OBSERVED = pulled directly from platform. CALCULATED = derived from observed values (show formula in source). INFERENCE = logical conclusion from observed data. ASSUMPTION = not verified — state what was assumed. DATA_NOT_AVAILABLE = attempted but couldn't get the data.

labeled_metric.description: A single metric with its value, display format, and provenance label.

value.description: Raw numeric value — the synthesizer does math on this.

source.description: Where this number came from. Required for OBSERVED (platform > page > metric). Required for CALCULATED (show formula).
